chapters.title: The First Mental Model
chapters.layout_note: A flow is the clearest grammar because this chapter teaches a transformation sequence.
chapters.summary: Show the beginner path from a confusing input to a usable mental model.
chapters.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A["Raw topic"] --> B["Key question"]
  B --> C["Mental model"]
  C --> D["First example"]
chapters.caption: A beginner learns faster when the first chapter turns confusion into one usable model.

chapters.title: How The Pieces Fit
chapters.summary: Show the topic as stacked levels so the learner can place new terms.
chapters.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  A["User goal"] --> B["Workflow"]
  B --> C["Core concepts"]
  C --> D["Tools or implementation"]
  D --> E["Tradeoffs"]
chapters.caption: The layers separate what the learner wants from how the system works.
